name: Methylation-supportive diet and lifestyle pilot lowered Horvath DNAmAge
summary: A small randomized pilot reported a saliva Horvath DNAmAge decrease relative to controls after an eight-week diet and lifestyle program.
statement: In a 43-person pilot RCT, an eight-week diet and lifestyle program was associated with a 3.23-year lower Horvath DNAmAge change compared with no-intervention controls.
population_or_model: Healthy adult males aged 50 to 72 years
quantitative_note: The abstract reports a 3.23-year DNAmAge decrease versus controls (p=0.018) and a within-treatment decrease averaging 1.96 years with p=0.066.
caveats: The trial was small, short, and limited to men.
The control group received no intervention, making expectancy and behavior-contact effects hard to isolate.
The intervention was multi-component, so no specific diet, supplement, sleep, exercise, or relaxation component can be credited.
The finding is a saliva DNA-methylation clock signal, not a functional aging outcome.
